metadata.title: SUPAC-MR: Modified Release Solid Oral Dosage Forms — Scale-Up and Postapproval Changes: Chemistry, Manufacturing, and Controls; In Vitro Dissolution Testing and In Vivo Bioequivalence Documentation
metadata.note: Despite the filename referencing 'IR' (immediate release), this PDF is the SUPAC-MR guidance for Modified Release solid oral dosage forms (September 1997, CMC 8). Rules are extracted accordingly.

rules.rule_text: Sponsors of NDAs, ANDAs, and AADAs who intend to change components/composition, site of manufacture, scale-up/scale-down, or manufacturing (process and equipment) of a modified release solid oral dosage form during the postapproval period must comply with this guidance.
rules.verbatim_quote: This guidance provides recommendations to pharmaceutical sponsors of new drug applications (NDAs), abbreviated new drug applications (ANDAs), and abbreviated antibiotic drug applications (AADAs) who intend to change (1) the components or composition, (2) the site of manufacture, (3) the scale-up/scale-down of manufacture, and/or (4) the manufacturing (process and equipment) of a modified release solid oral dosage form during the postapproval period.
rules.exceptions: This guidance does not address changes in the amount of the drug substance.

rules.rule_text: For SUPAC submissions, stability data from pilot scale batches will be acceptable to support the proposed change in most cases (except those involving scale up), and a commitment must be included to conduct long-term stability studies through the expiration dating period on the first or first three production batches with results reported in annual reports.
rules.verbatim_quote: In most cases (except those involving scale up), stability data from pilot scale batches will be acceptable to support the proposed change... A commitment should be included to conduct long-term stability studies through the expiration dating period, according to the approved protocol, on the first or first three (see text for details) production batches and to report the results in the annual reports.
rules.exceptions: Scale-up changes may require production-scale stability data.
cross_references: FDA Guideline for Submitting Documentation for the Stability of Human Drugs and Biologics (02/87)

rules.rule_text: Where stability data show a trend toward potency loss or degradant increase under accelerated conditions, historical accelerated stability data from a representative prechange batch must be submitted for comparison, along with all available long-term data on test batches from ongoing studies.
rules.verbatim_quote: Where stability data show a trend toward potency loss or degradant increase under accelerated conditions, it is recommended that historical accelerated stability data from a representative prechange batch be submitted for comparison. It is also recommended that under these circumstances, all available long-term data on test batches from ongoing studies be provided in the supplement.

rules.rule_text: The sponsor must provide appropriate justifications for classifying any excipient as a nonrelease controlling excipient in a modified release solid oral dosage form, and must identify the functionality of each excipient.
rules.source_section: Section III. Components and Composition — Nonrelease Controlling Excipient
rules.verbatim_quote: The sponsor should provide appropriate justifications for claiming any excipient(s) as a nonrelease controlling excipient in the formulation of the modified release solid oral dosage form. The functionality of each excipient should be identified.

rules.rule_text: For Level 1 nonrelease controlling excipient changes (changes unlikely to have any detectable impact on formulation quality and performance), the total additive effect of all nonrelease controlling excipient changes must not exceed 5% of the total target dosage form weight, with per-excipient limits: Filler ±5%, Disintegrant/Starch ±3%, Disintegrant/Other ±1%, Binder ±0.5%, Lubricant/Ca or Mg Stearate ±0.25%, Lubricant/Other ±1%, Glidant/Talc ±1%, Glidant/Other ±0.1%, Film Coat ±1%.
rules.verbatim_quote: Changes in nonrelease controlling excipients, expressed as percentage (w/w) of total formulation, less than or equal to the following percent ranges: Filler ±5, Disintegrant Starch ±3, Other ±1, Binder ±0.5, Lubricant Ca or Mg Stearate ±0.25, Other ±1, Glidant Talc ±1, Other ±0.1, Film Coat ±1... The total additive effect of all nonrelease controlling excipient changes should not be more than 5%.
rules.exceptions: Waiver of bioequivalence testing for changes involving only a different color, flavor, or preservative may be permissible per 21 CFR 320.22(d)(4).

rules.rule_text: Allowable excipient changes must be based on the original approved target composition, not on previous Level 1 changes. For products approved with only a range for excipients, the target value may be assumed to be the midpoint of the original approved application range.
rules.verbatim_quote: Allowable changes in the composition should be based on the original approved target composition and not on previous level 1 changes in the composition. For products approved with only a range for excipients, the target value may be assumed to be the midpoint of the original approved application range.

rules.rule_text: For Level 1 nonrelease controlling excipient changes, the filing documentation required is an annual report containing all information including long-term stability data.
rules.verbatim_quote: Annual report (all information including long-term stability data).

rules.rule_text: For Level 2 nonrelease controlling excipient changes (significant impact on quality and performance), multipoint dissolution profiles must be obtained in three other media (water, 0.1N HCl, and USP buffer at pH 4.5 and 6.8) for extended release, sampled at 1, 2, 4 hours and every two hours thereafter until 80% is released or an asymptote is reached. For delayed release, dissolution tests must be performed in 0.1 N HCl for 2 hours (acid stage) followed by USP buffer pH 4.5–7.5 (buffer stage) under standard conditions and two additional agitation speeds.
rules.verbatim_quote: Extended release: In addition to application/compendial release requirements, multipoint dissolution profiles should be obtained in three other media, for example, in water, 0.1N HCl, and USP buffer media at pH 4.5, and 6.8 for the changed drug product and the biobatch or marketed batch (unchanged drug product). Adequate sampling should be performed, for example, at 1, 2, and 4 hours and every two hours thereafter until either 80% of the drug from the drug product is released or an asymptote is reached.
rules.exceptions: In the presence of an established in vitro/in vivo correlation (IVIVC), only application/compendial dissolution testing need be performed.

rules.rule_text: For Level 2 nonrelease controlling excipient changes, the filing documentation required is a prior approval supplement (all information including accelerated stability data); long-term stability data to be reported in annual report.
rules.verbatim_quote: Prior approval supplement (all information including accelerated stability data); annual report (long-term stability data).

rules.rule_text: Level 2 nonrelease controlling excipient changes require one batch with three months accelerated stability data reported in the prior approval supplement and long-term stability data of the first production batch reported in the annual report.
rules.verbatim_quote: Stability: One batch with three months accelerated stability data reported in prior approval supplement and long-term stability data of first production batch reported in annual report.

rules.rule_text: For Level 3 nonrelease controlling excipient changes (likely to have a significant impact on quality and performance), a single-dose bioequivalence study is required. The bioequivalence study may be waived in the presence of an established in vitro/in vivo correlation.
rules.verbatim_quote: A single-dose bioequivalence study (3). The bioequivalence study may be waived in the presence of an established in vitro/in vivo correlation (6).
rules.exceptions: Waiver available if established IVIVC exists.
cross_references: FDA Guidance: Oral Extended Release Dosage Forms In Vivo Bioequivalence and In Vitro Dissolution Testing

rules.rule_text: For Level 3 nonrelease controlling excipient changes, stability requirements depend on availability of a significant body of information: if available, one batch with three months accelerated stability data in prior approval supplement and long-term stability of first three production batches in annual report; if not available, three batches with three months accelerated stability data in prior approval supplement and long-term stability of first three production batches in annual report.
rules.verbatim_quote: Significant body of information available: One batch with three months' accelerated stability data reported in prior approval supplement and long-term stability data of first three production batches reported in annual report. Significant body of information not available: Three batches with three months' accelerated stability data reported in prior approval supplement and long-term stability data of first three production batches reported in annual report.
rules.exceptions: Significant body of information for Modified Release is defined as existing after five years of commercial experience for the original modified release product, or three years for subsequent products utilizing similar drug release mechanism.

rules.rule_text: The sponsor must provide appropriate justifications (mechanism of drug release and manufacturing process) for classifying any excipient as a release controlling excipient in a modified release solid oral dosage form, and must identify the functionality of each excipient.
rules.source_section: Section IV. Components and Composition — Release Controlling Excipient
rules.verbatim_quote: The sponsor should provide appropriate justifications (i.e., mechanism of drug release and manufacturing process) for claiming any excipient(s) as a release controlling excipient in the formulation of the modified release solid oral dosage form. The functionality of each excipient should be identified.

rules.rule_text: For Level 1 release controlling excipient changes, the total additive effect of all release controlling excipient changes must not exceed 5% w/w of the total release controlling excipients in the original approved formulation, and the total weight of the dosage form must remain within the approved original application range.
rules.verbatim_quote: Changes in the release controlling excipient(s), expressed as percentage (w/w) of total release controlling excipient(s) in the formulation less than or equal to 5% w/w of total release controlling excipient content in the modified release solid oral dosage form... The total additive effect of all release controlling excipient changes should not be more than 5% w/w of the total release controlling excipients in the original approved formulation.

rules.rule_text: For Level 1 release controlling excipient changes, the filing documentation is an annual report (all information including long-term stability data).
rules.verbatim_quote: Annual report (all information including long-term stability data).

rules.rule_text: For Level 2 release controlling excipient changes in narrow therapeutic range drugs, a single-dose bioequivalence study is required; for nonnarrow therapeutic range drugs, no bioequivalence study is required. The bioequivalence study may be waived if an established IVIVC exists and the changes in release controlling excipients are within the range of the established correlation.
rules.verbatim_quote: Nonnarrow therapeutic range drugs: None. Narrow therapeutic range drugs: A single-dose bioequivalence study (3). The bioequivalence study may be waived in the presence of an established in vitro/in vivo correlation (6). Changes in release controlling excipients in the formulation should be within the range of release controlling excipients of the established correlation.
rules.exceptions: Waiver available if established IVIVC exists and excipient changes are within the IVIVC range.

rules.rule_text: For Level 2 release controlling excipient changes, narrow therapeutic range drugs require three batches with three months accelerated stability data in prior approval supplement and long-term stability of first three production batches in annual report; nonnarrow therapeutic range drugs require one batch with three months accelerated stability data in prior approval supplement and long-term stability of first production batch in annual report.
rules.verbatim_quote: Nonnarrow therapeutic range drugs: One batch with three months' accelerated stability data reported in prior approval supplement and long-term stability data of first production batch reported in annual report. Narrow therapeutic range drugs: Three batches with three months' accelerated stability data reported in prior approval supplement and long-term stability data of first three production batches reported in annual report.

rules.rule_text: For Level 3 release controlling excipient changes (addition/deletion of release controlling excipient, or changes exceeding 10% w/w of total release controlling excipient content), a single-dose bioequivalence study is required for all therapeutic ranges. The bioequivalence study may be waived in the presence of an established IVIVC and changes must be within the IVIVC range.
rules.verbatim_quote: A single-dose bioequivalence study (3). The bioequivalence study may be waived in the presence of an established in vitro/in vivo correlation (6). Changes in release controlling excipients in the formulation should be within the range of release controlling excipients of the established correlation.
rules.exceptions: Waiver available if established IVIVC exists and excipient changes are within the IVIVC range.

rules.rule_text: New manufacturing locations for site changes must have had a satisfactory current good manufacturing practice (cGMP) inspection.
rules.verbatim_quote: New manufacturing locations should have had a satisfactory current good manufacturing practice (cGMP) inspection.

rules.rule_text: A stand-alone packaging operations site change using containers/closures in the approved application may be submitted as a Changes Being Effected (CBE) supplement if the facility has a current and satisfactory cGMP compliance profile with FDA. If not, a prior approval supplement is recommended. The supplement must contain a written cGMP certification from the packaging facility and a commitment to place the first production batch on long-term stability studies.
rules.verbatim_quote: A stand-alone packaging operations site change, using container(s)/closure(s) in the approved application, may be submitted as a Changes Being Effected supplement. The facility should also have a current and satisfactory cGMP compliance profile with the FDA for the type of packaging operation in question before submitting the supplement. If the facility has not received a satisfactory cGMP inspection for the type of packaging operation in question, a prior approval supplement is recommended. The supplement should contain a written certification from the packaging facility stating that it is in conformance with cGMPs.

rules.rule_text: For Level 1 site changes (within a single facility with same equipment, SOPs, environmental conditions, and personnel), the filing documentation is an annual report only.
rules.verbatim_quote: Annual report.

rules.rule_text: For Level 2 site changes (contiguous campus or adjacent city blocks, same equipment/SOPs/personnel), the filing documentation is a Changes Being Effected (CBE) supplement (all information including accelerated stability data); long-term stability data reported in annual report.
rules.verbatim_quote: Changes Being Effected supplement (all information including accelerated stability data); annual report (long-term stability data).

rules.rule_text: For Level 3 site changes (different campus), a single-dose bioequivalence study is required and the filing documentation is a prior approval supplement (all information including accelerated stability test data); long-term stability data reported in annual report.
rules.verbatim_quote: A single-dose bioequivalence study (3). The bioequivalence study may be waived in the presence of an established in vitro/in vivo correlation (6)... Prior approval supplement (all information including accelerated stability test data); annual report (long-term stability data).
rules.exceptions: Bioequivalence study may be waived if established IVIVC exists.

rules.rule_text: Scale-down below 100,000 dosage units is not covered by this guidance. All scale-up changes must be properly validated and, where needed, inspected by appropriate Agency personnel.
rules.source_section: Section VI. Changes in Batch Size (Scale-Up/Scale-Down)
rules.verbatim_quote: Scale-down below 100,000 dosage units is not covered by this guidance... All scale-up changes should be properly validated and, where needed, inspected by appropriate Agency personnel.

rules.rule_text: Level 1 batch size change is defined as up to and including a factor of ten times the size of the pilot/biobatch, where the equipment is of the same design and operating principles, the batch is manufactured in full compliance with cGMPs, and the same SOPs, controls, formulation, and manufacturing procedures are used. Filing is via annual report.
rules.verbatim_quote: Change in batch size, up to and including a factor of ten times the size of the pilot/biobatch, where (1) the equipment used to produce the test batch(es) may vary in capacity, but are of the same design and operating principles; (2) the batch(es) is manufactured in full compliance with cGMPs; and (3) the same standard operating procedures (SOPs) and controls, as well as the same formulation and manufacturing procedures, are used on the test batch(es) and on the full-scale production batch(es).

rules.rule_text: Level 2 batch size change (beyond a factor of ten times the pilot/biobatch) requires a Changes Being Effected supplement including accelerated stability data and multipoint dissolution profiles; long-term stability data of first production batch reported in annual report.
rules.verbatim_quote: Changes in batch size beyond a factor of ten times the size of the pilot/biobatch where (1) the equipment used to produce the test batch(es) is of the same design and operating principles; (2) the batch(es) is manufactured in full compliance with cGMPs; and (3) the same SOPs and controls as well as the same formulation and manufacturing procedures are used on the test batch(es) and on the full-scale production batch(es).

rules.rule_text: For manufacturing equipment changes involving a different design and different operating principles (Level 2 equipment change), appropriate validation studies must be conducted and the filing is via prior approval supplement with justification for the change.
rules.verbatim_quote: If a manufacturer wishes to use manufacturing equipment that is not identical in every respect to the original manufacturing equipment used in the approved application, appropriate validation studies should be conducted to demonstrate that the new equipment is similar to the original equipment... Change in equipment to a different design and different operating principles.

rules.rule_text: For modified release solid oral dosage forms, consideration must be given as to whether the change in manufacturing equipment is critical to drug release (critical equipment variable).
rules.verbatim_quote: For modified release solid oral dosage forms, consideration should be given as to whether or not the change in manufacturing equipment is critical to drug release (critical equipment variable).

rules.rule_text: For manufacturing process changes, consideration must be given as to whether the change is critical to drug release (critical processing variable). Process change may be considered to affect a release controlling excipient only when both nonrelease and release controlling excipients are present during the unit operation undergoing a change.
rules.verbatim_quote: For modified release solid oral dosage forms, consideration should be given as to whether or not the change in manufacturing process is critical to drug release (critical processing variable). For purposes of categorizing the level of changes, process change may be considered only to affect a release controlling excipient when both types of excipients (i.e., nonrelease and release controlling) are present during the unit operation undergoing a change.

rules.rule_text: Level 3 manufacturing process changes (change in type of process, e.g., from wet granulation to direct compression of dry powder) require a single-dose bioequivalence study, three batches with three months accelerated stability data in prior approval supplement, and long-term stability of first three production batches in annual report.
rules.verbatim_quote: This category includes change in the type of process used in the manufacture of the product, such as a change from wet granulation to direct compression of dry powder... A single-dose bioequivalence study (3). The bioequivalence study may be waived in the presence of an established in vitro/in vivo correlation (6).
rules.exceptions: Bioequivalence study may be waived if established IVIVC exists.

rules.rule_text: For extended release dissolution testing (multipoint profiles), dissolution must be conducted on 12 individual dosage units using USP apparatus (basket, paddle, reciprocating cylinder, flow-through cell, or reciprocating disk), at 37±0.5°C, with dissolution volume of 500–1000 mL, and sampling at 1, 2, and 4 hours and every two hours thereafter until 80% is released or an asymptote is reached.
rules.verbatim_quote: Dissolution Testing: Extended release: Dissolution testing should be conducted on 12 individual dosage units for the changed drug product and the biobatch or marketed batch (unchanged drug product)... Temperature: 37±0.5ºC... Units To Be Tested: 12... Dissolution Volume: 500-1000 mL... Sampling Schedule: Adequate sampling should be performed, for example at 1, 2, and 4 hours, and every two hours thereafter until either 80% of the drug is released or an asymptote is reached.

rules.rule_text: For delayed release dissolution testing, drug release procedures in USP 23 NF 18 sections <711> and <724> must be followed, with dissolution tests performed in 0.1N HCl for 2 hours (acid stage) followed by USP buffer pH 4.5–7.5 (buffer stage), using standard conditions and two additional agitation speeds. For Apparatus 1 (basket): 50, 100, and 150 rpm; for Apparatus 2 (paddle): 50, 75, and 100 rpm. Multipoint sampling at 15, 30, 45, 60, and 120 minutes in the buffer stage until 80% is released or an asymptote is reached.
rules.verbatim_quote: Delayed release: For enteric coated drug products, drug release procedures described in USP 23 NF 18, sections <711> and <724> should be followed. When the guidance refers to dissolution testing in addition to application/compendial release requirements, the dissolution test should be performed in 0.1N HCl for 2 hours (acid stage) followed by testing in USP buffer media, in the range of pH 4.5-7.5 (buffer stage) under standard (application/compendial) test conditions and increased agitation speeds using the application/compendial test apparatus.

rules.rule_text: Dissolution profiles must be compared using the f2 similarity factor (f2 = 50 LOG {[1+1/n ∑(Rt-Tt)2]^-0.5 x 100}). An f2 value between 50 and 100 suggests the two dissolution profiles are similar. The average difference at any dissolution sampling time point must not exceed 15% between the changed and unchanged drug product.
rules.source_section: Glossary of Terms — In Vitro Dissolution Profile Comparison
rules.verbatim_quote: An f2 value between 50 and 100 suggests the two dissolution profiles are similar. Also, the average difference at any dissolution sampling time point should not be greater than 15% between the changed drug product and the biobatch or marketed batch (unchanged drug product) dissolution profiles.
rules.exceptions: An f2 value less than 50 does not necessarily indicate lack of similarity — a justification with additional data (e.g., 90% confidence interval analysis) may be submitted as part of a prior approval supplement.

rules.rule_text: For dissolution profile comparison using f2, only one point past the plateau of the profiles should be used in calculating the f2 value, and a correction for a lag time prior to similarity testing should not be performed unless justified. The reference for comparison should represent an average dissolution profile from at least three consecutive recent batches of the unchanged drug product.
rules.source_section: Glossary of Terms — In Vitro Dissolution Profile Comparison
rules.verbatim_quote: It is recommended that only one point past the plateau of the profiles be used in calculating the f2 value. A correction for a lag time prior to similarity testing should not be performed unless justified... An appropriate reference for this comparison should represent an average dissolution profile derived from at least three consecutive recent batches of the unchanged drug product (biobatch or marketed batch).

rules.rule_text: For modified release dosage forms, when an established in vitro/in vivo correlation (IVIVC) exists, changes in release controlling excipients in the formulation must be within the range of release controlling excipients of the established correlation, and only application/compendial dissolution testing need be performed.
rules.source_section: Glossary of Terms — In Vitro-In Vivo Correlation
rules.verbatim_quote: For modified release dosage forms, changes in release controlling excipients in the formulation should be within the range of release controlling excipients of the established correlation. In the presence of an established in vitro/in vivo correlation (6), only application/compendial dissolution testing need be performed.
cross_references: FDA Guidance for the Development, Evaluation and Application of In Vitro/In Vivo Correlations for Extended Release Solid Oral Dosage Forms, 1997

rules.rule_text: The biobatch for modified release solid oral dosage forms must be 10% or greater than the proposed commercial production batch, or at least 100,000 units, whichever is greater.
rules.verbatim_quote: Biobatch: The lot of drug product formulated for purposes of pharmacokinetic evaluation in a bioavailability/bioequivalency study. For modified release solid oral, this batch should be 10% or greater than the proposed commercial production batch or at least 100,000 units, whichever is greater.

rules.rule_text: Pilot scale for solid oral dosage forms is at minimum one tenth of full production scale or 100,000 tablets or capsules, whichever is larger.
rules.verbatim_quote: Pilot Scale: The manufacture of either drug substance or drug product by a procedure fully representative of and simulating that used for full manufacturing scale. For solid oral dosage forms this is generally taken to be, at a minimum, one tenth that of full production, or 100,000 tablets or capsules, whichever is larger.

rules.rule_text: A significant body of information for Modified Release Solid Oral Dosage Forms is a product-specific body of information that is likely to exist after five years of commercial experience for the original modified release solid oral drug product, or three years of commercial experience for any subsequent modified release solid oral drug product utilizing similar drug release mechanism.
rules.source_section: Glossary of Terms — Significant Body of Information
rules.verbatim_quote: Modified Release Solid Oral Dosage Forms: A significant body of information should include, for 'Modified Release Solid Oral Dosage Forms,' a product-specific body of information. This product-specific body of information is likely to exist after five years of commercial experience for the original modified release solid oral drug product, or three years of commercial experience for any subsequent modified release solid oral drug product utilizing similar drug release mechanism.
